I'm pretty amazed by this program. I've tried a ton of exercise dvds, but have never really gotten the eating part down. I've done Weight Watchers, Nutrisystem, and Diet to Go delivery service; all of which I've loved, but they didn't get me the results I was looking for. I've been pretty athletic most of my life and even when I've fallen off the wagon with exercise it hasn't been a problem reestablishing my fitness level, but the eating part...geez! A few years back, I discovered the Primal way of eating. (Primal is different from Paleo in that it allows full fat dairy if one can tolerate it. I gravitated toward that because I wasn't giving up cream in my coffee. Ever.) Anyhow, eating Primally helped me lose weight. Plus, I felt better. Not so fatigued. Whenever I'd go back to eating grains my heart would race and I just felt weird. The sugar part I had down pat. I'd discovered stevia before I discovered Primal style eating so I didn't worry at all about sugar content in my diet if I were doing the cooking. Although, for me, Primal style eating was healthier (again, for me!) I'd veer off the trail and go back to eating a SAD (Standard American Diet) diet just because I'd get lazy or work was overwhelming or I just wanted something quick and easy (fast food). So I remained on a yo yo with my weight. I lost and gained the same freaking 10-15 lbs constantly! It was so annoying. Plus, the weight just stayed in my belly. So, back in January I lost about 7 lbs. I had begun exercising with Jillian Michael's 30 Day Shred, then her Ripped in 30, but I got sick of the workouts after a while. Plus, they were pretty tough (although I got really strong). I wasn't seeing the fat fall off. I was ripped underneath a layer of fat, especially on my torso. So I got discouraged by the beginning of Feb. 2015 and just went back to eating crap. Publix cake etc. you name it I was eating it! I gained back 8 lbs from Feb to May this year. So that's when I decided to try 21 Day Fix. I'd seen an infomercial on QVC back in January, but I wasn't going to buy one more thing from QVC just yet. (There's so much good stuff on there it's addicting lol!) Plus, I was getting my Diet to Go delivery service (which was delicious btw) and I was still doing Jillian Michael's workouts. So after I was done with all that I thought let me see what this is about. I started the workouts on May 11th. The containers were like a monster in the closet for a while so I didn't even try to figure them out initially. I don't know why they were so intimidating to me and why I had so much anxiety about figuring out how to put them together to create my 3 meals and 2 snacks but they were (for me). Doing the workouts were easy breezy! I mean mentally. I was sore, of course, having to get back into a workout routine, but that's not a big deal for me. I'd just slap on some Biofreeze or Tiger Balm, knee braces and keep it moving lol! Autumn's workouts were so refreshing from the grueling Michael's workouts. They are awesome for someone who has been out of the working out loop and needs to ease back in. The first day Total Body Cardio Fix is tough if you haven't been exercising, but then day two Arms is such a welcome breather! It's still tough if you haven't been exercising, but I SO appreciated having a break from doing an all out total body workout with weights! Same thing with day 3 Legs. Day 4 Pilates is a freaking killer for me, but I love it! I can see it's going to get me the toning I want. Day 5 Cardio Fix is tough too. I like that it doesn't include weights. Sometimes I just want a break from weights. Then Day 6 Dirty 30 is awesome in that it's great full body weight training. The only workout I'm not thrilled about yet is the Day 7 Yoga. Right now, it's too strength focused and less relaxing/stretching focused. For me, just getting back on the workout wheel, I needed a lot more stretching. So what I've done is incorporated my 30 Minute Solutions Yoga for Beginners dvd on that day. It's way more relaxing and I get the stretching I need. Once more time has passed I'll probably alternate the Fix Yoga with my 10 Minute Solutions Yoga.

So on to the containers. After getting 5 days of working out under my belt, I wanted to figure out the containers. First, let me say I HAVE NOT BEEN HUNGRY AT ALL on this eating plan! That's huge!!! I did a ton of searches for meal plans and recipes. There's lots of stuff out there. I decided to make an egg muffins recipe, but then realized I liked having more control over being absolutely sure how many containers I'm eating. When stuff is all mixed together as in veggies and eggs etc I just didn't feel as confident about the number of containers I was eating with the recipe. So I've found that I like to keep things simple. For breakfast, I'll have an egg, 2 slices turkey bacon, a green container of broccoli and my coffee. I still use a couple of tablespoons of full fat cream in my coffee. Or I may have a yellow container of steel cut oatmeal with 1/2 purple container of cubed apple and blueberries mixed in. I'll include 2 slices of turkey bacon and my coffee. Lunch and dinner aren't hard at all. I absolutely LOVE how much protein I can have. I'll do a salad with ground turkey and and awesome Fix dressing for lunch and then yams, some type of steamed veggies and grilled chicken or ground turkey or ground beef for dinner. I even save half of my blue container so I can have parmesan cheese over my cauliflower for dinner. The one thing I like about this eating plan is that I can eat fruit and not just berries. There's a focus on the lower carb fruits with Primal eating which is fine, but sometimes I just want to savor a mango without feeling guilty. I found that I had been missing a variety of fruit, but now I incorporate it in my diet with zero problems! So after eating the 21 Day Fix way for 9 days from May 16 until May 24th I have lost a total of 5 lbs! I am amazed! I really don't understand how this can be. With the other eating systems the weight crawled off. I'd weigh in on a Friday and the next Friday I'd only have lost like .5 lbs. That was completely annoying and frustrating. With this program the weight has flown off! It's mind boggling! And get this-I've lost 5 lbs while eating a tablespoon or two grassfed butter in my coffee. (I was trying the bulletproof coffee thing for some days.) For me, that further solidifies my thinking that eating fat doesn't make you fat. It's carbs and insulin spikes that do that. Apparently, my carbs are in a good range with this eating plan. The other thing too is that I was eating steel cut oats and quinoa and fruits which increase carb content. I'm really floored and excited and thrilled! Never before have I been able to lose weight so quickly. Now I know some will say it's all water weight, which a lot of it could be so only time will tell as far as how much weight I'll lose on the program after this initial loss has leveled off. I'll update this as I go through the program. It'll be really interesting to see if I finally realize my goal of losing this layer of belly fat I've had forever. The rest of my body is fine. I don't have really any excess fat anywhere other than my belly which causes me not to fit into my clothes. So we'll see.

A few last observations...I absolutely LOVE the salad dressing recipes! I'm always depressed when I go on a diet and have to stick to oil and vinegar salad dressings. These dressings are ah-mazing! So far, I've tried the balsamic vinaigrette and the dijon vinaigrette.They are super tangy when you first make them, but they mellow out on the 2nd day once they've been refrigerated. I use my blender ball cup to make them. Makes whisking the ingredients together easy breezy. I just shake and pour each day when I'm ready to use some on my lunch salad. (Update 6/23-the Asian Citrus dressing is my new favorite and go to/staple. The lemon tarragon was okay and really a pain in the butt to eat with all the tarragon leaves swimming in the dressing. I won't make it again. The creamy herb dressing grew on me after I found a different use for it. It's more like a dip so I use it over ground turkey meat to give it a creamy, tangy flavor. I love it now. I think it's also great for folks who like to do veggie dips for snacks. It's definitely nice and creamy. I haven't tried making any of the treats. I find that I'm not craving sweets. Plus, sometimes for me, it's just a slippery slope when I eat a sweet when I'm following an eating plan. I also haven't swapped any yellow containers for wine. I usually like a glass of wine once or twice a week, but I'm finding that my tolerance level for even just a glass of wine has gone out the window. It tastes great going down, but then later I'm exhausted. So wine isn't on my radar at all.

On to Shakeology. I see some reviewers feel really strongly about the price of it. I can understand that, but when you break it down over 30 days it's literally $4 a day for a shake. I pay more for a grande Starbucks drink that's no where near as healthy! Right now, I can't afford to do Shakeology, but I did buy the sampler pack to see which flavors I like. For me, the strawberry, vanilla, vegan tropical strawberry and vegan chocolate were busts. If those were the only flavors they offered I wouldn't give Shakeology the time of day. The taste was just meh even with almond milk and mixed frozen fruit blended in it. When I got to the chocolate I was blown away! I don't know how in the world they got that chocolate to taste so divine, but they did and it's a hands down winner! The greenberry came in a distant second. It's not as sweet as the strawberry or vanilla. It has a nice balance to it. I'd alternate those flavors just so I wasn't drinking one day in and day out, but when I looked on the Beachbody website it looks like you can alternate the flavors, but just every other month. So, in other words, you'd get a one month supply of chocolate and then a one month supply of greenberry which wouldn't work for me. I'd want the option of being able to alternate those flavors on a day to day basis. So I really think I'll order the chocolate. I like the vitamin content in the shakes. The other protein shake I'm going to try is on Mark's Daily Apple website. It's called Primal Fuel. It's about $75 for a month's supply. I don't think it has the superfoods in it like Shakeology, but it has good fats and I believe some vitamin content in it. I'll see which of the two I like the best after giving them a good old college try.

I know this reveiw is long, (forgive my love of diary writing) but I hope it's been helpful to someone. :)

P.S. A couple other things I forgot to mention. Cat, the modifier, in the workouts is amazing! I followed her for the first two weeks and it was a life saver. Now I'm branching out with the more difficult moves. As for folks with knee issues (not major ones i.e. needing surgery etc.) investing in Triple Flex Nature Made vitamins may do the trick. My knees are creaky and make popping noises and get sore very easily after years of running track and cheerleading and doing exercise routines that weren't very good for them. I've been taking these pills for the very first time in my life for about a week or so and the difference is nothing short of amazing. The popping is minimized and it doesn't hurt to bend down when doing the surrender exercise on day 1. I'm so happy with these and will continue taking them. They're really cheap at Wal Mart for about $16. I didn't know that before and bought them online for about $8 more!

Update 6-27-15
I'm still going strong with the program. After tweaking my calorie range and figuring some other things out I'm back on the road to weight loss. Yay! So a few things I found out for myself is that the multiplier used in the original fix was too low for me. Multiplying by 11 to get your caloric baseline is apparently for strictly sedentary folks. If you work out fairly regularly (at least 3 days a week vigorously? I'm guessing), then you probably want to multiply by 12. I checked out the Extreme Fix book calculator and used 12 as a multiplier which put me in the 1500 range. My weight was in the 150s at that time, but I was following Autumn's program every day, but not losing weight in the 1200 range. In fact, I gained 1/2 lb which was frustrating. I was beginning to believe I was the only person in America that this program wasn't going to work for and I was sad about that. I LOVE this program and wanted it to work for me! I didn't really know what else I was going to do to figure out how to reach my weight loss goals if this didn't work. I'm sure eventually I would have figured it out, but Autumn's program is freakishly simple with these cute colored containers and her awesome workouts. It's the best eating and fitness program I've encountered. Plus, I didn't want to have to count macros and weigh food and all that. So I experimented with the 1500 calorie range and as soon as I did that the scale number started going down again. Hallelujah! So basically, I lost 5 lbs in the first 9 days and then my weight stalled until I figured out the info about the higher multiplier. I didn't figure that out until I was 27 days in. I think it was on Autumn's 'Ask Autumn' page that she said the original fix book will be revamped to include those multipliers for people who are more active.

So anyway, I was losing weight again, but then went on a wine tour and blew everything to heck lol! I got back on track for about 5 days, then decided I wanted to try the 3 Day Quick Fix (I'll write a separate review for that). The quick fix was a success, but after doing more research, I realized that it was mainly water weight that I lost. I was really surprised when I didn't see my percent body fat go down really at all. I did lose 3.4 lbs doing the quick fix so I'd highly recommend it for anyone preparing for a special event. Autumn is definitely right when she says that's what you should use it for. It shouldn't be looked at as a weight loss tool IMO though because good weight loss comes from losing fat period. I finally get that the scale number truly doesn't matter. It's the composition of that number that matters and what folks should want is lean muscle and a good percentage of body fat depending on the look you're going for. That depends on whether you're a body builder, a bikini model like Autumn or someone who doesn't care about looking ripped, but do want to fit well in their clothes. That's an important lesson I've learned from doing this program among a score of other things that would make this review even longer than it already is. So that's it! I won't update this review again, but I hope it's been helpful with the troubleshooting tips on calculating calorie ranges etc. I'm making a life long change because of Autumn. I enjoy food now and cooking. I don't feel guilty about eating. I feel amazing and I sleep well on a daily basis. Most importantly my relationship with food has healed. I no longer will be afraid to have the piece of birthday cake at a party or indulge occasionally because now I know what to do to undo dietary damage. I'm really going for an 80/20 or 90/10 balance as far as eating goes. Sometimes you want to indulge, but that should be a treat and not how you live your life daily if you want to have any quality of life and good health. I can see myself eating this way forever. Thank you Autumn!!!

3 Day Quick Fix
Apparently I can’t write a separate review for the same program so here’s my review of the 3 Day Quick Fix. :)
I read the little pamphlet when I got everything and was like I'm not ever doing that. I despise 'quick fixes' that have you starving and miserable! I was like whatever. But after my little wine tour eating fiasco (see my review above for that story) I rethought my stance on it. I looked at the meals and did a bunch of research i.e. Google searches ;-) and surmised that it's basically carb depletion. I know tons of people probably know about it, especially those in the fitness industry, but I've never heard of it in my entire life. Now I understand why Autumn uses it to get ready for a show or whatever. It doesn’t help you lose a ton of body fat, but you do lose a bunch of water weight. I didn’t know this before I did the fix. I decided to try it out of curiosity and also because I wanted to see if I could fast track my fat loss. First off, it’s totally doable. I wasn’t hungry at all since you’re eating protein all day long. I loved that part. Before I started the Fix I was following Primal style eating, which I still love, so I didn’t miss that I wouldn’t have fruit or dairy (although Primal eating does allow dairy, but I digress) and just the 2 yellows throughout the day. If you think you’ll be miserable if you can’t have fruit for 3 days, then this fix isn’t for you.

My thoughts after completing the 3 Day Quick Fix- I did some research on body building forms and realized my 3.4 lb weight loss was basically due to my body burning away through my glycogen stores since I wasn’t eating as many carbs each day. I spot checked one day and found that I had eaten about 53 net carbs for the day, 220ish grams of protein and about 50 grams of fat. Don’t quote me, but those numbers are the numbers I can recall. So it makes sense that I lost all of that. It was mostly water. My body fat percentage went down like maybe .1 after the 3 days. I was bummed at first because the whole reason for me doing the quick fix in the first place was to lose fat. I didn’t know about how the body uses glycogen etc. until afterward. Doing the fix was still beneficial though. I was able to take a break from all the dairy I’ve been eating by way of cottage cheese and Greek yogurt. I liked the break from fruit. I’m not a person that just has to have fruit. I also liked taking a break from a yellow container surprisingly. I will say that after 3 days I was ready for some variety back in my diet. I completed 2 full days of eating on the regular plan while checking my weight and percent body fat on both days. I thought I’d gain all the water weight back, but I’m happy to report that I didn’t! I won’t lie. Seeing a smaller number on the scale has a positive effect regardless if it’s the number I really want to see. (I’m more concerned with having a lower body fat percentage vs. the scale weight number.) Because I’d lost the 3.4 lbs, that put me in the lowest calorie bracket. I immediately switched to the 1200 bracket after eating on the regular 1500 plan for the 2 days following the quick fix. Once I switched to the 1200 bracket, the weight has been coming off again. More importantly, I’m seeing my body fat percentage fall which is really what I want. I want to preserve my lean muscle and lose this fat! Losing the fat helps with sculpting the body.

So here are some final thoughts on the quick fix:
-use it if you have an important event coming up and want to lose a few pounds (remember you won’t lose fat weight, but water weight)
-easy, easy, easy, but you have to prep for it
-prepping is non negotiable
-still getting two yellow containers makes it doable
-getting to eat a ton of protein makes it even more doable
-you MUST season your food! I don't know how anyone can do this without adding seasoning! I found some great salt free seasonings from TJ Maxx. (Y'all know they always have the good stuff. This is no Mrs. Dash. Just say NO to Mrs. Dash! It's flavorless and gross!) I'm using Blue Crab Bay Co. salt free seasoning for seafood. Really you can use it on anything. Maybe not beef, but you're not eating beef on this quick fix anyway. It goes well with all the meat I'm eating which is chicken, fish and ground turkey.
-I hate egg whites! I don't know who in their right mind would want to eat egg whites unless they're body building or have cholesterol problems or for some other issue. They take forever to cook and taste like nothing! The only time I want to eat egg whites is via a meringue on top of a pie! (After trying egg whites and sweet potatoes though they’re growing on me. They add volume to the potatoes. I eat them just like it’s oatmeal that way. Yummy!)
-I cheated and did add touches of salt to all of my meats. Just sayin'. I'm not prepping for a competition but I still got great results.
-Oh yeah, which brings me to add that I salt brined my chicken breasts before cooking them. Ha! Totally forgot about that. I have a few non-negotiables. Eating dry, unsalted, leathery tasting chicken breasts is one of them. I'm just not going to suffer through that. The salt brining made them juicier than they would be, but they weren't off the charts juicy which is par for the course for chicken breasts. It did make eating them A LOT better. I seasoned them with ground sage (the best poultry seasoning ever!), ground garlic and smoked paprika. It was actually good. Not great, but not so bad where I was questioning why am I eating this?
-I didn’t take the coconut oil microwaved in water. I just put it in my coffee. It was fine that way, but I did add ½ tbsp. of half and half to it. I’m just not there yet with the black coffee.
-lean ground turkey isn’t tasty without salt :(
-drinking tea after most meals made me feel more satisfied. I wasn’t hungry after the meals. I think it was more psychological. My fave teas to drink were Oolong, Sweet Ginger Peach by Revolution, and Brew la la's Organic Chocolate Mint Caffeine Free tea. That one tasted like dessert once you add some stevia to it.
-Check out Autumn’s story on Youtube. She tells how she got to Beachbody. I just love her! She’s such an inspiration!

Here are the final stats:
Starting weight- 151.2 lbs, 31% body fat, 24 BMI

Ending weight – 148.4 lbs, 31% body fat, 23.5 BMI

I hope this is helpful to someone! Good luck!
top_rant.title: Amazing Program!

top_rant.text:
I take my time to review things like this. These type of machines can be an expensive item and we are all consumers who rely on other people's reviews to get an idea of whether or not it is worth the hard earned dollars we work so hard for. Paper can hold anything you write on it, or in this case the screen. But believe me when I say, this company really delivers on its promises. Please allow me to explain. I consider anything worth over $50 to be something to review after ample time has been taken to evaluate, not as soon as you get it and it looks cool and pretty nor during what I call the "Honeymoon" phase of 2 to 4 weeks. You know, that time you're pumped but then it becomes just another piece of something in your house occupying space. Yeah that phase. Most people cannot afford a $400 emergency so to even spend $50 or more on something deserves the most honest reviews in my opinion. Once you get in to a couple of $100's then I believe it is my duty to do my due diligence and have enough experience with the product to actually convey judgement.

I do not usually get excited with things but I honestly believe this is one of the best machines, if not the best machine, for the average Joe and Jane out there! Mind you, I waited for Prime Day 2020, to get mine cheaper, again most of us cannot afford such purchases on the fly, but this machine is worth every single penny! Good thing is from what I have seen in the last two months the company, LifePro, does a lot of promotions in both Amazon and their website. The free item promos are listed on their website just FYI. ;-) But I decided to buy at Amazon because I knew if it was bad, I could return it through Amazon. Needless to say, nobody can separate me from this machine! LOL.

This machine works! They have many style and sizes with some models being more simple and then the top of the line models like my Rumblex 4D. I'm a pretty big guy with health issues and I needed something that could hold my weight, not fall off from it (that is why I decided on the one with a flat surface),and since I was going to take a conscious gamble, I wanted it do everything under the Sun that these machines could do! Also, I wanted something that was comfortable enough to not take too much space in the house as my house is not a big one. The Rumblex 4D seem like a perfect fit. Here's some back story...

I recently had an injury where I hurt my back and I have been in treatment for close to a year. I got stuck at one point and nothing seemed to be working. The doctors were like, well you need to lose weigh. But how?!!! I couldn't even walk a straight line much less do exercise! So instead of accepting defeat, I decided that I needed to look into alternatives that even my doctors, would not necessarily think of or recommend "because they do not have the studies to recommend it". Yeah. "Thanks doc!". Through my journey, I've learned that yeah doctors are great and all but they have a very narrow way of doing things. Very limited views and if it doesn't make them money or it comes from big pharma, "They don't have enough studies to recommend it". They make more money when you're not cured, or at least feel well enough to not have to be there every week or every month. So being an informed man, and willing to take calculated risks or calculated gambles, I was looking for solutions "outside the box".

I've done Chiropractic treatments, the usual physical therapy (multiple times), electric stimulation, manual stimulation, pills, rest, changes in diet, more pill changes, you name it. But I resist the idea of injecting anything or having surgery to "fix" the problem when they cost hundreds and thousands of dollars, not to mention the pain and suffering, plus missing work for a 50/50 chance. No thank you! So I said to myself, "all of the things I am doing are helping to some extent but they only get me so far. I need something new and different from what my doctors are willing to do. I don't think surgery is a risk I am willing to take now". So I started reading. I looked into Vibration plates and similarly named machines. I looked at the ones in and outside of the Amazon market, and literally found hundreds of these these machines. But in all honestly, most looked flimsy, came from China with no warranty and most looked like a fire hazard or an accident waiting to happen. You can tell when something is well made, when its photoshopped or is just plain old crap.

Then there were the machines that cost quite a few of hundred dollar bills and a few more that cost a whopping couple thousand dollars. Needless to say, once I saw one that I recognized from a Spa/Wellness Center I used to go to, I got even more curious. The machine I recognized was one of in the thousands. A Big ol' thing but everyone seemed to love getting on it at the Spa/Wellness Center. So after viewing and reading literally hundreds of products and reviews both in and out of Amazon, discarding the ones I couldn't afford because of the "thousands of dollars" price tag, I noticed LifePro. At first I thought it was too good to be true. Great reviews, it seemed to do the same if not more than some of the "thousands of dollars" machines and only for a couple of hundred dollars but with something the others did not have, and this is the kicker, LIFETIME WARRANTY! I said, "Wait a minute! They stopped doing those Lifetime warranties when my mother was a child in the 60's and 70's! LIKE WHAAAAAAT?!". I couldn't and wouldn't believe it and so I went on detective mode. LOL.

I did more and more digging. I compared the machine (Rumblex 4D) with the high priced tagged ones and others in a similar price rage. I was trying to find out if any of the reviews posted were faked or paid for. See if any of them were used somewhere else as a review. Nothing suspicious. It all seemed legit but I resisted to believe any of it. I'm in my 30's but I've been buying things online way before even Facebook was created, so I know a thing or two about scams. But come to find out, it was all true. No fake reviews, no paid reviews, nothing shady about it. Plus, the Rumblex 4D seem to do everything the big ones did and even more than some of them. This company, this product, this is no scam. It is a legitimate business with a legitimate product. I was pleasantly surprised, skeptical still but pleasantly surprised.

So to my point. Yes, I know that for a lot of us it's hard to come up with $300 or $400 dollars. I ended up waiting for a sale and paying for it with a mixture of gift cards and credit cards. But hey, listen, it is WORTH IT!!! The machine really works. I just recently went up to level 10 with all the functions on (4D) this week. I've seen changes and I'm still on level 10 from 60!!! Mind you, I am hurt and the company does advise to be careful and start slow and easy and work your way up. That shows they care. Because slow first actually works best!

It's best you start slow, but believe me it is still working. DON'T JACK IT UP! Take it slow!!! Trust me, I know it works because those first few days when you urinate, OMG!, it will smell horrible! Like 10 times worse than normal but it's because it is helping you clean out that lymphatic system! Listen, I didn't even really know what the lymphatic system was, much less how it worked or why it was important. Trust me, aside from getting relief for my pains, just the benefits of the lymphatic system drainage IS WORTH THE MONEY! You won't believe those first few weeks, and sometimes, maybe longer, like me. You will see a color change, a smell change. I mean this is basically cleaning and helping your body flush out all kinds of yucky stuff in all of your insides. Everything because of all that stimulation!

It's been two months now. My headaches which were almost daily, have diminished I'd say by 95%! I'm loosing weight, slowly but surely. My skin looks better and I get less allergies. I guess that lymphatic system drainage and whole body exercise and vibration really was what my body needed to start healing itself. All that old added stuff to meds that stays behind after I took it is now being flushed out. I thought it was out, it wasn't. You know what I am talking about, those "inactive ingredients", that stuff keeps accumulating in your body. Now that I am flushing it out, I am feeling better than I have in a long long time. My leg muscles are stronger and my balance has improved! I am not saying this is a cure for all your ailments, but this is no "snake oil" either my friends! It does exactly what it says it does.

The construction is sturdy and I am a "big guy", hopefully soon a used to be a big guy! :-) My mother who has fibromyalgia, among many other ailments, has seen how great it has been for me and she decided to give it a try. It relaxes her so much she is actually able to sleep better. It will not cure her fibromyalgia but at least it is helping her with balance and sleeping. That to me alone is worth the money, to be able to help my mom. If you can afford it, I say wholeheartedly, buy it. It has been a game changer for me and my disabled mother whom I take care of.

Companies like LifePro, who make a high quality product, that is fairly priced, does sales on their products and stand behind it with a NO COST TO YOU LIFETIME WARRANTY, ARE UNHEARD OF IN THE 21ST CENTURY! I do not know of any other company that does or even pretends to offer this. Because those supposed " lifetime warranties" out there they charge you ridiculous shipping and handling fees, some higher than the actual product price because the manufacturer is in some other country or the say "Lifetime" means a certain amount a years they expect the product to last like 3 years. Which is not Lifetime Warranty as advertised in my opinion. LifePRo is in the Good Ol' USA! Free shipping to send or receive through warranty if you ever have an issue. You paid what you paid for the machine at purchase, then you're done, FOR LIFE! It is totally worth it! And believe me I never write reviews like this!

Also, Joel, the guy who is I think the owner, that is always answering questions here and there, he seems to be very in touch with his customers. I sent questions and got answers quickly. AND I MEAN QUICKLY! And who gets to say they got a direct response from the owner of a company like this anymore?!

I love this personal touch, "hands on" business approach. It reminds me of when companies made things to last and cared about building quality over quantity, and more about the customers and investing in their loyalty rather than quick profits in volume. It reminds me of a time companies had more integrity and this speaks very well of LifePro and their leadership!

They also have a video database which I honestly still haven't used aside from the basic stuff just because I am still under treatment, but when I am ready, trust and believe I am going in full throttle! Further down the road I am thinking of getting one of their massage "guns". I mean, everything seems to be of quality so instead of buying the ones they sell in the fitness stores, or from places like Wal-Mart and Target, which seem like really good deals but do not offer the Lifetime Warranty or even anything beyond 90 days at best, I'll wait to get it from LifePro. BEST MONEY YOU WILL EVER SPEND BECAUSE WE NEED OUR GRIP STRENGTH FOR ALMOST EVERYTHING! But yeah I can go on and on with High Praise of the overall and long term benefits these will do for you in every day life. I am able to carry more groceries in each hand 1 TRIP FOR SURE NOW! I am able to change a tire better and patch tires better because of my grip strength. BUY THESE! TRAIN WITH THEM SMART AND SAFELY… WARM UP! AND THEN PUSH YOURSELF, CHALLENGE YOURSELF! But they are FUN, VERY EFFECTIVE, EFFICIENT, AFFORDABLE, VERY, VERY PRACTICAL and they will last you YEARS UPON YEARS! They truly are the under rated, unsung hero in your Strength Training / Training and Fitness Goals! BUY THEM! Buy the ones you want in JUST ONE QUANTITY! And just switch hands when you use one.

If you are a Man and never grip trained or you are a regular weight lifter START WITH THE 100LB TRAINERS! BUY THE 100LB TRAINERS! BECAUSE YOU WILL NEED TO WARM UP SOME DAYS! YOU WILL EVENTUALLY NEED THEM AND YOU WILL BE THANKFUL YOU HAVE THE 100LB TRAINERS TO WARM UP WITH!

Women I recommend the ones under the 100lb Trainers. Because the 100lb Trainers will be very advanced for any woman who can close them. So women be smart, train safe and use the lightest ones. top_rant.text:
Ok! So I thoroughly reviewed a lot of the comments (mainly the 2 stars and below) about this product before deciding to buy it. I'll try to address all the issues raised using my experience. Hopefully wahoo is reading this. I just completed a 56 mile ride with it.

Delivery!
Amazon was incredible. I ordered this product at about 5pm on saturday and paid for their guaranteed 24hr delivery and it go to my door by 7pm sunday (2 hrs before their 9pm deadline). I honestly wasn't expecting them to make that delivery deadline but they did and that's just amazing! Great job Amazon!!!

Aesthetics
This is a very beautiful product. Hooked it up to my handle bars and just looked gorgeous. You can very easily remove it from the casing that holds it and reattach. It is very sturdy. Rode on some pretty rough sections and no matter how much the handle bar vibrated, the computer remained sturdy. Great job there Wahoo! Also you can attach the computer to your stem using the easy (provided) rubber band - which is pretty sturdy too - or using zip ties. Good job there Wahoo.

Customization
Excellent customization. You can create all the screens you want and see all the data you want with this. Altitude, temperature, climbs, cadence, speed, HR, HR zones, you name it, you can customize a display for it so you can actually see what you're doing in real time! This is pretty cool. Limitation is that you can only do this with the Wahoo app. If you connect this to MMR or any other device, you just get basic info.

Usability
I'll give it a 3 stars. You have to press the buttons several times to get the display, especially the start/pause button. Then again, you can always reprogram that and set it to another button. But for a brand new product, I wanted all the buttons to work with one press of the button! Its brand new for goodness sakes!!! Otherwise, its easy to use, once you set it up. The set up takes a little bit of a learning curve. Wahoo has done a great job posting some awesome videos on youtube and their website that will walk you through that.

iPhone Battery Life
I started my ride this morning with 89% battery life, and spent a total of 4 hours and change between ride, regrouping and rest stop. I turned my iPhone screen off. I still got display on RFLKT which was awesome. I turned off all other background apps on the phone so the only one running was WAHOO fitness app. At the end of ride, I had about 21% power left and I have an iPhone 4s. So battery power is not too shabby, considering you're using GPS etc.

CONNECTIVITY
In this regard, I'll give the product a 1.5 stars. So there are several issues. First, it is very easy to pair this device to the Wahoo App. You can also connect it to Map My Ride directly (I love MMR), however it does not allow you to customize the screen with MMR. So you're better off using Wahoo App so you can customize you screen and see more data. Second. Connectivity to other devices. My Wahoo HR monitor paired very easily with the app without any problems. I decided to order and use the Wahoo HR monitor to maintain consistency in platform, hoping that this will result in less connection issues. Third! The RFLKT has an ant+ bridge which paired well with my bontrager duo trap speed/cadence sensor. But here's the kicker. As soon as I started my ride this morning, it completely dropped all of the signals from my speed/cadence sensor (and even the heart rate monitor). I assumed this may have been due to the connectivity (I had my phone in my back pocket), so I just rode to the meeting point for my ride without any data, after 2 miles into the ride. TERRIBLE! TERRIBLE!! TERRIBLE!!!. I'm 5'10, 155 lbs. Not a lot of body mass to block bluetooth signal, especially with iPhone in my left pocket, not the middle. Further, I had to completely close the app on the iPhone and reopen it for it to pair with the sensors again. This fixed the problem. To take care of the blue tooth connectivity, I just tucked my iPhone in my arm-cooler sleeve. This fixed that problem. I never had any dropped signals for the next 20+ miles. RFLKT worked like a charm and I was totally loving it. Picked up HR, as well as speed/cadence. Worked like a charm. At rest stop, I didn't turn it off, just paused the workout, took the RFLKT with me so its close to the iPhone. It continued to display HR, which was great. At the start of ride, I started the workout again and it wouldn't pair with my speed/cadence monitor! Arrrghhh!!! So aggravating!!! So I had to stop the ride, save it, turn off the app, turn it back on and pair it all over again with all the sensors. Once that was done, I had no problems. Again, worked like a charm! My hypothesis is that once all sensors are paired, you have to keep all of them within a certain distance of the iPhone so they remain paired. All good and fine, except that's not physically possible during any ride. You can't sit there with your bike the whole time and you can't go to the bathroom with your bike or enter the restaurant with your bike .... you get my drift. However, the part that Wahoo needs to work on is ensuring that you can easily pair all the sensors again once you start. I hate having to stop the workout, save it, close the app, reopen it, just to pair all my devices. It completely defeats the purpose of having bike computer. All my workouts are now segmented into 4 parts - My ride to meeting point (lost some data due to loss of connection with iPhone in my back pocket), my ride from meeting point to rest stop (had to stop app, save data, close app, reopen app, reconnect sensors in order to record), and my ride from meeting point to my home (again, had to stop app, save data, close app, reopen to connect sensors). So I cannot do any meaningful analysis of my ride performance with this segmented information!!!! That is completely vexing and just straight up frustrating!! WAHOO, YOU NEED TO IMPROVE CONNECTIVITY, SO THAT IF SIGNAL IS LOST BETWEEN DEVICES AND RFLKT, IT CAN BE EASILY PAIRED AGAIN WITHOUT HAVING TO STOP WORKOUT, CLOSE APP, REOPEN, RECONNECT AND START A NEW RIDE!!! THIS PART COMPLETELY KNOCKS THE PRODUCT DOWN!

So summary!
1. Its a beautiful product! Gorgeous. You'll love the way it looks on your bike
2. Its easy to attach to your bike and use
3. Takes a little bit of playing around, to set it up and customize the screens to see data you want, but once that's done, its pretty awesome how much data you can collect and see during your ride
4. It pairs with bluetooth and ant+ devices
5. Plays very well with other apps. You can very easily download your workout to any of the supported apps (MMR, Strava, etc) and there are a lot of apps you can download to.
6. Battery life is ok. But if you're doing long rides, get a battery charger for your iPhone.

Problems!
1. Connectivity is a problem! If you are going to use this device, don't put it in your back pocket. A very easy solution is to keep your iPhone in an arm band and strap it on your arm. This will solve the "back pocket connection" problem.
2. MAJOR PROBLEM is that once you loose connectivity with ant+ devices, you may have to save your workout, turn app off, turn it back on, and repair all devices again before you start. This will lead to segmented data during your ride, which depending on what you're looking at, makes the data almost useless! If WAHOO can fix this connectivity problem, I'll definitely to give this product 4 stars (not 5 stars because I have to get an arm band to hold my iPhone close to device).

So there you have it. I'm going to use this a few more times and see how it performs and also see if there are any tricks I can find (like using an arm band) that may fix some of the shortcomings of the product. If I continue to have problems with ant+ connectivity after breaks on my ride, I'm going to return this product. I need all my data in one piece, not segmented. That said, its pretty awesome to have your iPhone as your bike computer and save the money you'll spend on Garmin and such!

UPDATE 9/6/14
So, I'm upgrading my review of this product to 3 stars with a potential for 4 stars. Just finished a metric century using the RFLKT. I did a couple of this different. First. I followed WAHOO suggestion and put the my iPhone in my saddle bag on my bike. Second. Instead of connecting my WAHOO HR monitor via bluetooth, I connected it via ant+. With these modifications, I got excellent connection between the RFLKT+ and my HR and Speed & cadence monitor. Also, I had excellent, uninterrupted bluetooth connection between my iPhone and the RFLKT+. Absolutely no dropped signals, nothing! Also at rest stops, I was able to pause my workout (still have to push the button a couple of times), but when I wanted to start my ride, it reconnected with my HR and cadence monitors seamlessly. No need to stop, save, close, reopen app. I'm very pleased about this. I didn't even have to use the armband I bought and carried just in case. Battery life was awesome too. I had 100% battery when I started and it ran great. I could have finished the ride with my iPhone, but I turned on my mophie charger at mile 48 with 27% battery life left. So this is great!

WEATHER
This is a huge plus for WAHOO. It poured during my ride and the RFLKT+ worked seamlessly during the ride - no interruptions, not shut down, no dropped signal, nothing!! Also the HR monitor worked seamlessly even though I was dripping wet, down to my cycling shorts, shoes (I mean dripping wet).

Summary,
If this product continues to work the way it did today, its definitely a keeper!!!! :-)
top_rant.title: Awesome in some aspects, Horrible in some aspects. Easy fixes by Wahoo can make this a great product

top_rant.text:
Bestguarder WG-80 4X50mm HD Digital Night Vision Binocular.

First I want to say that I don't usually do Reviews. I've bought literally hundreds of Products here in Amazon, and this is ONLY my second Review; (although the majority of products bought, performed to my satisfaction). But This Product & This Company's Customer Service simply AMAZED ME! I Pride myself in buying "Made in the USA"; but this Company went ABOVE & BEYOND my expectations! I'm Proud to own this Chinese Product! (Even we in the US could learn a thing or two about Customer Service & Satisfaction from Chinese Companies like this!

CUSTOMER SERVICE:
Bestguarder has GREAT CUSTOMER SERVICE ; courteous, caring, eager to listen to customer's concerns, and who STAND BEHIND THEIR PRODUCT. I have to APOLOGIZE to The Manufacturer for initially giving this Product a 3*** rating. Although in my initial review I praised THE EXCELLENT optics and Night Vision Capabilities of the WG-80 4X50; gave it the 3 star rating for quality control issues on a perceived defect on my part which was TOTALLY MY FAULT for NOT reading the manual (Can't read small print as I used to). After the review, they contacted me, telling me they would immediately forward my concerns to their Production Dept. and asking me to give their Product another chance, they would make it Right! WOW Am I GLAD I did! They also sent me a PDF Manual & Quick Guide which was awesome.

So after receiving the second pair of Binoculars (Reading the PDF Manual - New Technology to me), and putting them to the test; I'M COMPLETELY BLOWN AWAY at it's excellence vs. Price! But before I get to my Review; I like TO HELP Prospective Buyers who are debating whether to get these Binoculars; with some FACTS, that will be HELPFUL in making their decision a good one.

TO REVIEWERS:
First I like to challenge buyers or potential buyers of this Product to BE HELPFUL to potential buyers when you write a review: If you LIKE OR LOVE the Product, please don't just give your rating & say: "They're great" or "Awesome buy" etc. without expressing the WHY you are giving your 4 or 5 star rating, and mentioning ALL that you liked about it; PLEASE try to HELP prospective buyers like yourself to make an informed decision. If you DON'T LIKE OR HATE the Product; don't just give your 1 or 2 star rating & say: "They suck" or "Garbage" or "Cheap Chinese Crap" or "Rip Off" without explaining WHY?

One reviewer "Prairiedogdance" Stated: "While the person I gave them to likes it, I just found out these are NOT true binoculars" and goes on to say "It's a Rip Off" & "Misleading" & "Chinese Scam"; saying he could have gotten a real bi-vision night vision; (PLEASE GO DO IT...so you can see the vast DIFFERENCE), and although the person he gave them to LIKES THEM, he disparages the Product & gives it 2 stars WITHOUT TALKING PERFORMANCE VS PRICE! Although in essence these Binoculars do have only Optics in one side (the other being the IR Illuminator), Your eyes can't tell the difference (You can individually focus each eyepiece to each individual eye's need), plus focus with the Center Wheel the Subject matter, just like regular Binoculars; all this while watching in a TFT Screen!

As a Customer, sometimes I'm not only LEARY of Ratings like that, but sometimes I think it's ill intention-ed competition trying to knock off the Product. So giving this product a 1, 2 or even 3 stars, just because you compare them (clarity & field of view) with regular daytime field binoculars or Gen 3 NVG (Night Optics PVS-14 Gen3 Auto-Gated Night Vision - $8.800) it's NONSENSE! Do you know how many Positive 5* Reviews it takes to cancel out a Negative 1-2* Review, and the damage your carelessness can infringe on a Manufacturer, without even GIVING THEM a chance to FIX the Problem? (A bad unit can slip through ANY Company's Quality Control). It's what happens afterward that matters! If Customer Service is rude, uncaring, unapologetic & don't want to fix the issue to YOUR SATISFACTION; by all means, give them a 1* Review!

KNOW YOUR PURPOSE & EXPECTATIONS
There are some things customers must understand about NV products, so they have a realistic understanding of their expectations; and NOT knock a product like this with bad ratings, just because they DON'T UNDERSTAND the product, and what their expectations should be. Sometimes people get into video games like "Call Of Duty" and expect that all night vision should perform like Military Gen 3. They are PRIMARILY a budget NV device! The rest are just perks. They do their job, and they do it EXTREMELY WELL!

I'm Ex Military, so I do HAVE experience with NVO. (Old Guard - Gen 1/2), and you should understand what you are buying; and the PRICE POINT vs PERFORMANCE of this NV Device; which to me, CAN'T be beat. In my opinion if its a choice between IR technology and Gen1 technology, I take IR technology any day of the week & twice on Sunday. Yes it's active illumination technology, but in areas of near or complete darkness they simply work better.

When you buy Night Vision, unless you want to take a mortgage on your house for some Military Gen 3 ($4.000 to $8.000), you better UNDERSTAND the limitation of (Gen 1 - Analog Technology - $300 to $800). and ALL Night Vision Optics like this one (Digital - Active Illumination Technology) which has many advantages over Gen 1. (You can use them in day or night); and have perks like built in camera & video capabilities. With Gen 1 you have LIMITATIONS: You can ONLY use them @ night, very narrow field of view, fish-eye, blurry edges & black spots - normal for intensifier tubes, temporary night blindness (Monocular); and NO Camera or Video Capabilities.

For starters the maximum life of the intensifier tubes is 2.000 hrs. under optimal conditions (Not exposing them to daylight or even bright artificial lights like streetlights), as this damages the tubes and shortens their life. So this limits their use if you want them to last. Infra Red Digital Technology doesn't have this problem, you can use them day or night. Besides that, image quality is better, (Comparable & better than some Gen 2). With Gen 1, all you get is NV, that's it. No perks, bells or whistles; like being able to take pics & video (with sound) in HD, or being able to zoom in & out (4X Optical, 5X Digital), GPS, Time stamp, & all the other goodies on your pics & videos. And you don't have to worry about your Intensifier tubes reaching the end of it's service life if you use them too much or expose them to light.

I own the Sight Mark Ghost Hunter 1X24 Gen 1 Goggle Kit sold here in Amazon; and the difference between those and these Bestguarder WG-80 4X50 Binoculars is like day & night. With these, you get a TFT display; which give you WAY larger Field of View (Like looking @ a large square screen in front of your eyes); and 50mm Lens; compared to 24mm for Sight Mark (very narrow field of view - Like looking through a roll of toilet paper).

PRODUCT REVIEW:
I purchased this item, after having purchased the "Bestguarder 6x50mm HD Digital Night Vision Monocular". Because I was IMPRESSED with it's NIGHT VISION CAPABILITIES; and being able to take PICTURES & VIDEO WITH SOUND; which is the main purpose for my purchase. I was amazed how well & clear I could see at night with just moon light or VERY low ambient light ! They performed as good or better than Gen 2 NV Goggles (2.000+). Night became DAY, with every detail even in the very dark bushes. I could even see buildings & objects @ 300 meters, which I could NOT see with my naked eyes. The optics & Night capabilities of this device are EXCELLENT. I was blown away by how GOOD they performed even WITHOUT the IR (IR0). You can focus each eyepiece individually, and adjust them in or out for interpupillary separation. Also the main center wheel can be adjusted for subject focus (after zooming in or out). Alright NOW I own the night...dirt bags look out!

Each optic has it's place; I use the Sight Mark NV Monocular mounted on my Fast PJ Helmet for hands free operation when I'm in pursuit of the bad guys ( Security Work), while handling my radio, weapon & other gear @ the same time. But when I'm ON STAKEOUT, and taking PICTURE or VIDEO EVIDENCE, without having to multitask with my hands; DEFINITELY The WG-80 is MY OPTION! Way BETTER Image & Optics, GREATER Field of View, COMFORT with two handed operation, BUILT LIKE A TANK, and also GREAT ERGONOMICS without temporary night blindness. In my line of work I use both; (Can't sell my truck to buy PVS-14's), but I LOVE THESE compared to my Gen 1. If you're a Preper like me, or work Law Enforcement, Security, or a Hunter, Camper, Hiker, Explorer, or just want to Observe Nature, and capture those moments when day turns to night & the critters come out to play THESE ARE THE ONES for You!

PICTURE THIS:
* You work in Law enforcement or Security . You're conducting a night Surveillance OP, while the dirt bags are doing their drug deals, pimping, stealing & every other nasty thing you can think of....you pull out your WG-80's and Document the Evidence with HD Picture & Video. You capture the dirt bags, D. A. presents evidence in Court...scumbags go to prison & BESTGUARDER just MADE YOU A HERO and a BIG FAT Bonus or pay raise!

* Your Rich husband or wife is being unfaithful. They're @ their very dark rendezvous where they think NO one is watching...you pull out your WG-80's and CAPTURE THE MOMENT...Divorce Court...BESTGUARDER just MADE YOU RICH with no strings attached!

* You're out HIKING and a storm, fog etc. take you off your trail to find cover for the night. Next day you're LOST & CAN'T find your way back to Civilization...you pull out your WG-80's and take some PICTURES with GPS TAG @ your surroundings, you take the Micro SD Card out, & place it in your Smart Phone (If you're phone doesn't have Picture GPS Tagging), find reception & send GPS PICTURES to your friends & loved ones....BESTGUARDER JUST SAVED YOUR LIFE!

I know...I'M TOO EXITED ABOUT THIS PRODUCT...But I promise I don't work for, or are related in ANY WAY to Bestguarder, (I'm a Verified Purchaser), and PROUD TO OWN this CHINESE MADE Product!... Bottom line: They have EXCELLENT NV Capabilities, and AWESOME OPTICS! QUALITY VS. PRICE is second to none! I will be POSTING Videos & Pictures soon; as right now we're in the middle of winter here in Southern CA. and it's been raining cats & dogs (we needed it); and wouldn't be fair to post gloomy pictures. Also I have to find out about Amazon's Policies and also how to do it...SO, WHAT ARE YOU WAITING FOR? TAKE THE PLUNGE!!!

top_rant.text:
I just got a Crosman Fury: Even though it's a piston and not a springer it still is hold sensitive. Making sure to put my fingers on the stock exactly the same each shot I'm better with the Fury the first day than with my Gamo Silent Cat after 3 months!

I still love my Silent Cat, but the Fury is: heavy metal and well built; 4x scope not 3x; no iron sights but no iron sights to interfere with the view through the scope; the barrel is smaller so most pellets have to be pushed in not just dropped in loosely; tap to break barrel open and a bit harder to cock; nicer trigger; the noise is an old fashioned air pop instead of a spring twang so it's louder than the Cat but way, way easier to bullseye over and over with any pellet I choose, even cheap Destroyers! All for half the price of the Gamo! I love both, but the Gamo is for silent 4am de-raccooning of my roof, while the Fury is for bullseyes on paper indoors.

PELLET TEST UPDATE: 30’ (measured with a tape measure and no rest). I tried:
Benjamin Hunting Hollow Points (750 tin of domed hollow point)
Beeman Silver Arrow (pointed, huge 11.5g)
Crosman Premier Hollow Points
Gamo Bone Collector (domed hollow point)
Crosman Premier Super Match (flat top wadcutter)
Diablo Basic (green tin of flat top wadcutter)
Crosman Wadcutter Premium Grade (tin of 250, premium not premier)
Gamo Red Fire (red polymer plastic spiked tip)
Crosman Destroyer
Crosman Destroyer EX
Gamo Tomahawk
30 year old flat top (Crosman or Daisy) wadcutters I found in a drawer
PLUS: a bunch of other pointed and evil looking hunting pellets I had laying around.

FIRST THE BAD: Gamo Tomahawk, Destroyer, Destroyer EX were all pretty horrible. The Destroyer EX were slightly better than the regular Destroyer, but just barely. There were a bunch of pointed pellets I had that were also horrible. The only pointed pellet that did okay were the crazy Gamo Red Flame pellets with the red plastic tip. They were pretty darn good actually! They fit REALLY tight into the barrel, which I think is a good thing.

THE BETTER: The only pellet I had that did (a smidge better) than the Gamo Red Flame were the Crosman Premier Hollow Points. These are domed hunting pellets with a hollowed out point. I still wasn’t pleased, and I actually found a handful of 30 YEAR OLD Crosman (or Daisy) flat top wadcutters which were AWESOME (in comparison). So I ordered a bunch of flat top wadcutters to test up against the Crosman Premier Hollow Points.

THE BEST SO FAR: Here is where it gets weird. After testing 7 different pellets with two different holding styles (more or less artillery hold) I have a clear winner, a clear second place that is not really for accuracy and a few different pellets that either win 3rd place or don’t depending on how I hold this rifle.
WINNER: The Benjamin hunting hollow points (in the 750 pellet tin) was by FAR the best pellet. Twice in a row I shot 5 pellets and ALL 5 PELLET HOLES IN THE PAPER TARGET TOUCHED! This is a domed hunting pellet with a hollow point. You can’t argue with 5 holes all touching on the paper target with no rest at 30 feet using two different rifle holding techniques and no rest either time.

SECOND PLACE: this is a weird one. The Beeman Silver Arrow 11.5g pellets are HUGE, long pellets that are really heavy. Each single shot of these looks like two holes touching because either the pellets go sideways or are tearing the paper (they’re pointed for killing and NOT flat wadcutters for cutting clean circular wads of paper out of paper targets). I shot them into some wood and they are NOT going sideways. They’re just beasts that shred the paper. If you’re going to kill a raccoon or something I’d use these: they’re accurate and devastating. The tears in the paper make it hard to tell what exactly is going on: shredded bullseye after two shots. LOL. I think the points of impact may be wider than some of the third place winners, but only slightly. In an animal you WANT this type of tearing for an instant kill. For paper targets it’s just a mess. Two shots and it looks like the target got hit by a shotgun. Neat, but hard to tell precision-wise what is going on exactly.

THIRD PLACE: Crosman Premier Super Match (flat top wadcutters) won with the most touching holes both times with different holds.

POSSIBLE THIRD/FOURTH PLACE DEPENDING ON HOLD OF RIFLE: Crosman Premier Hollow Points either came in third or worst depending on hold. Gamo Bone Collector and Diablo Basic (green tin wadcutters) either came in fourth or last depending on hold. Depending on how YOU hold your Whisper Cat you may do better with a Bone Collector or Diablo Basic than MY third place Crosman Premier Super Match. In fact three large raccoons were dispatched at 4am using this rifle and Gamo Bone Collectors (after owning this rifle for less than 2 days and not having shot pellet/bbs in the past 20 years or so). ANOTHER aside about the Bone Collector: unlike the photo somebody posted showing a merely bent penny, I actually SHATTERED A PENNY INTO TWO PIECES with a Gamo Bone Collector Pellet at 30 feet.

Stay away from my “First the Bad” list and anything that is pointed (except for Red Flame which were pretty awesome and left nice wadcutter type circular holes in paper). I’d Concentrate on trying:

Benjamin hunting hollow points (in the 750 pellet tin)
Crosman Premier Super Match (flat top wadcutters)
Gamo Bone Collector
Diablo Basic (in the green tin can)
If you need to kill something large, go for the Beeman Silver Arrows, they just make a SHREDDED MESS of the paper targets, so it’s not as much fun for precision. If a wild boar or grizzly broke into my house and I could only shoot it once with a pellet it would TOTALLY be the Beeman Silver Arrows. I think I’d end up using the rifle as a club quickly afterwards, but at least the first shot would be the worst for the beast. LOL.
WHAT’S NEXT: Well, I planned on buying a bunch more flat top wadcutters to test against what I ASSUMED would be the winners of the testing when I opened up all these tins of pellets today. Now, I’m not so sure. I still want to try a bunch of fancier wadcutters (the ones made in Germany) but I’m thinking I should be testing more domed and domed hollow points.

I lined up the pellet tins in a row and started at one end used each one. Then I changed my hold and started at the other end and went backwards. I fired 5 pellets each. On a few if I had an obvious flyer that was either weird or totally just my fault I’d take a 6th shot. I did this all very quickly, and I did NOT adjust the scope or where I was firing. I just aimed at the bullseye center and shot 5. It was the grouping (closeness) of the holes in the paper that I cared about, not if I was high or low or left or right. With all of that any and every time I used the Benjamin HP (750 per tin) ALL THE HOLES IN THE TARGET TOUCHED!
The BEEMAN Silver ARROW 11.5g pellets aways shredded the target with touching shredded ragged holes after two or three shots making it difficult to tell where the last two or three shots went (but they went through the original shredded shots).
ALL FIVE of the Benjamin HP (750 per tin) holes on the target touched and could fit within two of the Silver Arrow holes. The Crosman Premier Super Match and Premier Hollow Points *may* mostly fit into an almost as small area, but both that 1 or 2 flyers outside the main group every time. Even when I took 6 shots they spread out a little more on the paper.

Still love this rifle!

There are 3 less raccoons in my neighborhood. Domed Gamo Bone Collector pellets are accurate and can punch almost all the way through a 2x4. My shoulder muscle has gotten used to cocking it so no ringing arm/hand syndrome. It doesnt smoke anymore and I'm STILL USING THE ORIGINAL SCOPE WHICH IS DEADLY ACCURATE x 3.

EDIT: found higher scope mount rings.

Millett See-thru weaver-style 1" .22 smooth SE00014. Let's me use iron sights and scope on my Gamo Silent Cat. I have to take the scope covers off so they don't block the iron sights.

The iron sights are also set to almost their lowest setting. I could easily get more clearance if I added shims under the scope tube before I dropped it onto the bottom of the rings (Before clamping the tops on).

To keep the scope from sliding backwards after each shot I just jammed a brass screw into the scope stop hole (see my photos). It still worked after about 100 shots: iron sights work close, scope works and doesn't creep and 95% of the obstruction from the silencer and iron sights is gone!

The ultra light weight pellets that come with this rifle go so fast they break the sound barrier and crack like a real rifle.

I've ordered some super heavy 11.5 pellets. Update: shot number two was with Beeman Silver Arrow 11.57g pellets and it was so quiet all you could hear was the spring twang.

The iron sights show in the scope a little and the scope is too low to allow you to use the iron sights with it on. There are probably higher mount rings available that would raise the scope. My old ten pump daisy is set up that way now.

PYRAMYD AIR shipped it and it arrived THE SAME DAY!!!!! I live close by in Michigan though.

Buy heavier ammo for quieter and more accurate shooting.

After the two shots outside I took it in my basement and marked off 30 feet with a tape measure. In the sitting position the scope was to the right, I adjusted and 2nd and 3rd shots were closer. The 2nd shot had a lot of smoke come out (friction of spring ignites extra lube in the compression chamber and it made a lot of smoke--I was kind of freaked out). 3rd shot smoked only a little. 4th shot (after adjusting scope again) was dead center and no smoke. I STOOD UP and freehand shot the rifle and the 5th shot actually went through the hole of the 4th shot.

I called it a GREAT NIGHT and went to bed (happy).

I'm ordering a bi-pod off Amazon cheap (just for kicks) and am searching for the ever elusive 11mm dovetail extra high scope mount rings with the SEE-THRU holes that will (hopefully) let me use the scope and the sights at the same time.

YES: the scope gets in the way of the sights. Normally I wouldn't care but the sights have cool fiber optic thingies that seem fun. I might just buy a regular set of extra tall mounts and drill a big hole in each one on my drill press.

Oh the RECOIL! I shot actual firearms (real guns with real bullets): Ar-15, Ruger 10/22, Savage 22, Glock 19, S&W .357. All of those guns except for the .357 have no recoil--only muzzle rise. The .357 stings my hand.....THIS GAMO stings my hands just a little and my face. These springers have forward and backward recoil like slapping your hand on a table top real hard: ringing. LOL! I read about that but I didn't believe it.

About the smoke coming out: 9 out of 10 people on message boards say it's normal, their rifles still smoke after 2000 shots, you can't do anything about it because opening the gun kills the warranty,etc. Then there's always that 1 guy that says even once smoking (or dieseling as they call it) means the gun is destroyed. Well: shots 2 and 3 smoked. 4 didn't and 5th went through the same hole as 4th even though I moved from sitting to standing. I'm not (overly) worried.

Oh, indoor shots 1-5 were Gamo bone collectors. I chose them because they're a good medium weight 7.xxg and thought they'd be easier during break-in/sighting indoors than those INSANE Platinum PBA they give you 50 of.

***I fired two shots (a Platinum PBA & Beeman Silver Arrow 11.5g)outside plus the 5 indoors (7.xg Bone Collectors) and the smoke was almost all gone***

My other pellet rifle is a 20 year old 10-pump Crossman with no recoil ;)

Oh: unlike the Crossman 10-pumps you can ONLY use pellets in this Gamo (no BBs).

We'll see if I can find elevated see-thru mount rings or the scope rattles to pieces first. I actually liked the scope, it's not as nice as my Nikon scope I (got free) and slapped on my old ten pump but its fine for now.

Also just realized: I never ran a cleaning cloth thru the barrel before shooting. I was just so excited! Probably couldn't hurt to put a dry cloth through the barrel to get rid of any manufacturing gunk. Order and try lots of pellets and remember that a change in pellets (and thus weight) will mean you'll have to re-adjust the sights/scope.

FUN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

-mike from Detroit
top_rant.title: PELLET TEST and Get taller scope mount

PLUS: a bunch of other pointed and evil looking hunting pellets I had laying around.

FIRST THE BAD: Gamo Tomahawk, Destroyer, Destroyer EX were all pretty horrible. The Destroyer EX were slightly better than the regular Destroyer, but just barely. There were a bunch of pointed pellets I had that were also horrible. The only pointed pellet that did okay were the crazy Gamo Red Flame pellets with the red plastic tip. They were pretty darn good actually! They fit REALLY tight into the barrel, which I think is a good thing.

THE BETTER: The only pellet I had that did (a smidge better) than the Gamo Red Flame were the Crosman Premier Hollow Points. These are domed hunting pellets with a hollowed out point. I still wasn’t pleased, and I actually found a handful of 30 YEAR OLD Crosman (or Daisy) flat top wadcutters which were AWESOME (in comparison). So I ordered a bunch of flat top wadcutters to test up against the Crosman Premier Hollow Points.

THE BEST SO FAR: Here is where it gets weird. After testing 7 different pellets with two different holding styles (more or less artillery hold) I have a clear winner, a clear second place that is not really for accuracy and a few different pellets that either win 3rd place or don’t depending on how I hold this rifle.
WINNER: The Benjamin hunting hollow points (in the 750 pellet tin) was by FAR the best pellet. Twice in a row I shot 5 pellets and ALL 5 PELLET HOLES IN THE PAPER TARGET TOUCHED! This is a domed hunting pellet with a hollow point. You can’t argue with 5 holes all touching on the paper target with no rest at 30 feet using two different rifle holding techniques and no rest either time.

SECOND PLACE: this is a weird one. The Beeman Silver Arrow 11.5g pellets are HUGE, long pellets that are really heavy. Each single shot of these looks like two holes touching because either the pellets go sideways or are tearing the paper (they’re pointed for killing and NOT flat wadcutters for cutting clean circular wads of paper out of paper targets). I shot them into some wood and they are NOT going sideways. They’re just beasts that shred the paper. If you’re going to kill a raccoon or something I’d use these: they’re accurate and devastating. The tears in the paper make it hard to tell what exactly is going on: shredded bullseye after two shots. LOL. I think the points of impact may be wider than some of the third place winners, but only slightly. In an animal you WANT this type of tearing for an instant kill. For paper targets it’s just a mess. Two shots and it looks like the target got hit by a shotgun. Neat, but hard to tell precision-wise what is going on exactly.

THIRD PLACE: Crosman Premier Super Match (flat top wadcutters) won with the most touching holes both times with different holds.

POSSIBLE THIRD/FOURTH PLACE DEPENDING ON HOLD OF RIFLE: Crosman Premier Hollow Points either came in third or worst depending on hold. Gamo Bone Collector and Diablo Basic (green tin wadcutters) either came in fourth or last depending on hold. Depending on how YOU hold your Whisper Cat you may do better with a Bone Collector or Diablo Basic than MY third place Crosman Premier Super Match. In fact three large raccoons were dispatched at 4am using this rifle and Gamo Bone Collectors (after owning this rifle for less than 2 days and not having shot pellet/bbs in the past 20 years or so). ANOTHER aside about the Bone Collector: unlike the photo somebody posted showing a merely bent penny, I actually SHATTERED A PENNY INTO TWO PIECES with a Gamo Bone Collector Pellet at 30 feet.

Stay away from my “First the Bad” list and anything that is pointed (except for Red Flame which were pretty awesome and left nice wadcutter type circular holes in paper). I’d Concentrate on trying:

Benjamin hunting hollow points (in the 750 pellet tin)
Crosman Premier Super Match (flat top wadcutters)
Gamo Bone Collector
Diablo Basic (in the green tin can)
If you need to kill something large, go for the Beeman Silver Arrows, they just make a SHREDDED MESS of the paper targets, so it’s not as much fun for precision. If a wild boar or grizzly broke into my house and I could only shoot it once with a pellet it would TOTALLY be the Beeman Silver Arrows. I think I’d end up using the rifle as a club quickly afterwards, but at least the first shot would be the worst for the beast. LOL.
WHAT’S NEXT: Well, I planned on buying a bunch more flat top wadcutters to test against what I ASSUMED would be the winners of the testing when I opened up all these tins of pellets today. Now, I’m not so sure. I still want to try a bunch of fancier wadcutters (the ones made in Germany) but I’m thinking I should be testing more domed and domed hollow points.

I lined up the pellet tins in a row and started at one end used each one. Then I changed my hold and started at the other end and went backwards. I fired 5 pellets each. On a few if I had an obvious flyer that was either weird or totally just my fault I’d take a 6th shot. I did this all very quickly, and I did NOT adjust the scope or where I was firing. I just aimed at the bullseye center and shot 5. It was the grouping (closeness) of the holes in the paper that I cared about, not if I was high or low or left or right. With all of that any and every time I used the Benjamin HP (750 per tin) ALL THE HOLES IN THE TARGET TOUCHED!
The BEEMAN Silver ARROW 11.5g pellets aways shredded the target with touching shredded ragged holes after two or three shots making it difficult to tell where the last two or three shots went (but they went through the original shredded shots).
ALL FIVE of the Benjamin HP (750 per tin) holes on the target touched and could fit within two of the Silver Arrow holes. The Crosman Premier Super Match and Premier Hollow Points *may* mostly fit into an almost as small area, but both that 1 or 2 flyers outside the main group every time. Even when I took 6 shots they spread out a little more on the paper.

Still love this rifle!

There are 3 less raccoons in my neighborhood. Domed Gamo Bone Collector pellets are accurate and can punch almost all the way through a 2x4. My shoulder muscle has gotten used to cocking it so no ringing arm/hand syndrome. It doesnt smoke anymore and I'm STILL USING THE ORIGINAL SCOPE WHICH IS DEADLY ACCURATE x 3.

EDIT: found higher scope mount rings.

Millett See-thru weaver-style 1" .22 smooth SE00014. Let's me use iron sights and scope on my Gamo Silent Cat. I have to take the scope covers off so they don't block the iron sights.

The iron sights are also set to almost their lowest setting. I could easily get more clearance if I added shims under the scope tube before I dropped it onto the bottom of the rings (Before clamping the tops on).

To keep the scope from sliding backwards after each shot I just jammed a brass screw into the scope stop hole (see my photos). It still worked after about 100 shots: iron sights work close, scope works and doesn't creep and 95% of the obstruction from the silencer and iron sights is gone!

The ultra light weight pellets that come with this rifle go so fast they break the sound barrier and crack like a real rifle.

I've ordered some super heavy 11.5 pellets. Update: shot number two was with Beeman Silver Arrow 11.57g pellets and it was so quiet all you could hear was the spring twang.

The iron sights show in the scope a little and the scope is too low to allow you to use the iron sights with it on. There are probably higher mount rings available that would raise the scope. My old ten pump daisy is set up that way now.

PYRAMYD AIR shipped it and it arrived THE SAME DAY!!!!! I live close by in Michigan though.

Buy heavier ammo for quieter and more accurate shooting.

After the two shots outside I took it in my basement and marked off 30 feet with a tape measure. In the sitting position the scope was to the right, I adjusted and 2nd and 3rd shots were closer. The 2nd shot had a lot of smoke come out (friction of spring ignites extra lube in the compression chamber and it made a lot of smoke--I was kind of freaked out). 3rd shot smoked only a little. 4th shot (after adjusting scope again) was dead center and no smoke. I STOOD UP and freehand shot the rifle and the 5th shot actually went through the hole of the 4th shot.

I called it a GREAT NIGHT and went to bed (happy).

I'm ordering a bi-pod off Amazon cheap (just for kicks) and am searching for the ever elusive 11mm dovetail extra high scope mount rings with the SEE-THRU holes that will (hopefully) let me use the scope and the sights at the same time.

YES: the scope gets in the way of the sights. Normally I wouldn't care but the sights have cool fiber optic thingies that seem fun. I might just buy a regular set of extra tall mounts and drill a big hole in each one on my drill press.

Oh the RECOIL! I shot actual firearms (real guns with real bullets): Ar-15, Ruger 10/22, Savage 22, Glock 19, S&W .357. All of those guns except for the .357 have no recoil--only muzzle rise. The .357 stings my hand.....THIS GAMO stings my hands just a little and my face. These springers have forward and backward recoil like slapping your hand on a table top real hard: ringing. LOL! I read about that but I didn't believe it.

About the smoke coming out: 9 out of 10 people on message boards say it's normal, their rifles still smoke after 2000 shots, you can't do anything about it because opening the gun kills the warranty,etc. Then there's always that 1 guy that says even once smoking (or dieseling as they call it) means the gun is destroyed. Well: shots 2 and 3 smoked. 4 didn't and 5th went through the same hole as 4th even though I moved from sitting to standing. I'm not (overly) worried.

Oh, indoor shots 1-5 were Gamo bone collectors. I chose them because they're a good medium weight 7.xxg and thought they'd be easier during break-in/sighting indoors than those INSANE Platinum PBA they give you 50 of.

***I fired two shots (a Platinum PBA & Beeman Silver Arrow 11.5g)outside plus the 5 indoors (7.xg Bone Collectors) and the smoke was almost all gone***

My other pellet rifle is a 20 year old 10-pump Crossman with no recoil ;)

Oh: unlike the Crossman 10-pumps you can ONLY use pellets in this Gamo (no BBs).

We'll see if I can find elevated see-thru mount rings or the scope rattles to pieces first. I actually liked the scope, it's not as nice as my Nikon scope I (got free) and slapped on my old ten pump but its fine for now.

Also just realized: I never ran a cleaning cloth thru the barrel before shooting. I was just so excited! Probably couldn't hurt to put a dry cloth through the barrel to get rid of any manufacturing gunk. Order and try lots of pellets and remember that a change in pellets (and thus weight) will mean you'll have to re-adjust the sights/scope.

FUN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

-mike from Detroit
